title: Piña Colada Chia Pudding
summary:
Check out this Healthy Teaching Kitchen recipe video featuring a refreshing Piña Colada Chia Pudding, made with nourishing ingredients like chia seeds, pineapple, and light coconut milk. This tropical-inspired treat is easy to prepare, rich in fiber, and perfect for breakfast, a snack, or a light dessert. 

Prep: 5-10 minutes | Chill time: 6 hrs or overnight | Total: 6 hrs 10 minutes | Yield: 4 servings | Serving Size: 1/2 cup

Ingredients:
1 cup canned light coconut milk
¼ cup chia seeds
1 tablespoon agave syrup
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
1½ cups crushed pineapple, drained
2 tablespoons unsweetened shredded coconut

Directions:
1. In a medium bowl, whisk together coconut milk, chia seeds, agave syrup, and vanilla until well combined.
2. Cover bowl and refrigerate for 6 hour (or overnight).Then, stir chia seed pudding to avoid clumps.
3. Divide half of the crushed pineapple evenly into four small serving cups (about 2 tablespoons each).
4. Spoon chia pudding over the pineapple, then top with the remaining crushed pineapple.
5. Sprinkle each serving with ½ table spoon unsweetened shredded coconut and serve, or keep chilled until ready to eat. 

Recipe keeps up to 3 days in the fridge.

Recipe Notes:
•Right before serving, layer with pineapple purée. If preparing ahead, store the purée and chia pudding separately to avoid a watery layer on top.
•For a refreshing frozen twist, blend frozen pineapple with ½ cup frozen banana slices for extra creaminess.

Nutrition Facts Per Serving: Calories: 200 | Total Fat: 13g | Saturated Fat: 8g | Sodium: 35mg | Total Carbohydrate: 20g | Dietary Fiber: 5g | Protein: 3g

For more recipes, please visit https://www.nutrition.va.gov

title: No One-Size-Fits-All: VA Mental Health Treatment for Veterans
summary: When it comes to mental health care, there’s no one-size-fits-all approach. Among VA's evidence-based therapies, medication management, talk therapy, group sessions, and residential treatment, you can find the treatment combination that’s tailored to your needs and lifestyle. Talk with your VA provider to find what works for you.

title: VA Mental Health Care: Personalized Treatment for Every Veteran
summary: VA mental health care is built for every Veteran, whether you are just getting out of the military or are an older Veteran facing new challenges. Explore treatment options, learn about evidence-based therapies, find local VA resources, explore self-help tools, and see how to connect with peer support or professional care when you’re ready.

title: Mental Health Support for Veterans: Your Local VA Care Options
summary:
If you’re a Veteran looking for mental health support, VA is here to help you explore your local care options. Discover nearby treatment for PTSD, depression, anxiety, and substance use disorder. Local residential rehabilitation programs are also available. You don’t have to figure this out alone. Find the care that feels right for you.  

Find VA locations near you: https://www.va.gov/find-locations/

title: The Rise of Remote Work: Effects on California's Labor Market
summary: The pandemic accelerated widespread adoption of remote work, which has transformed California's labor market. In this report, we look at four key job groups where remote work has become common—technology, finance and accounting, business and government operations, and sales and marketing. Our review traces recent weakness in the state's labor market for these jobs to how the rise of remote work has restructured where Californians work, where they choose to live, and where employers look to hire.
